revision.comment: Bot: remove [[Category:Sn crud]] (crud category cleanup) [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/23985667504|commit triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
[[ja:檜尾神社]] {{ill|Hinoki Shrine|fr|ja|zh|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hinoki Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ZH|qid=Q224881|1=JA}} is a {{ill|Jōyō kanji|en|Jōyō kanji|qid=Q875368|lt=Jōyō kanji|1=Jōyō kanji}}, or a {{ill|Chinese characters|en|Chinese characters|qid=Q8201|lt=Kanji|1=Chinese characters}} used in writing the {{ill|Japanese language|en|Japanese language|qid=Q5287|lt=Japanese language|1=Japanese language}}. It is a secondary school kanji.{{Cite web |title=KANJI-Link |url=http://www.kanji-link.com/en/kanji/grade/ |access-date=2023-06-02 |website=www.kanji-link.com |language=en}} ==See Also== * {{ill|List of jōyō kanji|en|List of jōyō kanji|qid=Q841128|lt=List of jōyō kanji|1=List of jōyō kanji}} ==Overview== {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name=Hinō Jinja (檜尾神社) | image=File:Hinoo jinja shrine 14.jpg | location={{ill|Shiga Prefecture|en|Shiga Prefecture|qid=Q131358|lt=Shiga Prefecture|1=Shiga Prefecture}} {{ill|Kōka, Shiga|en|Kōka, Shiga|qid=Q847584|lt=Kōka City|1=Kōka, Shiga}} Kōnan-chō, Ikeda 54 | coordinates={{Coord|34.90013888888889|N|136.2012222222222|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | enshrined_deity={{ill|Amatsuhiko-hiko-hono-ninigi-no-mikoto|de|Amatsuhiko-hiko-hono-ninigi-no-mikoto|ja|天津彦彦火瓊々杵尊|zh|天津彦彦火瓊々杵尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amatsuhiko-hiko-hono-ninigi-no-mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135578967|1=Ninigi-no-Mikoto}} | annual_festival={{ill|Vernal Equinox Day|en|Vernal Equinox Day|qid=Q1049141|lt=Vernal Equinox Day|1=Vernal Equinox Day}} | main_hall=3×1-bay nagare-zukuri with hinoki bark roof | rituals={{ill|Rice Planting Ritual on Vernal Equinox Day|de|Pflanzritual am Frühlings-Tagundnachtgleiche|ja|春分の日 御田植神事|zh|春分节 插秧仪式|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rice Planting Ritual on Vernal Equinox Day|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135578970|1=Rice Planting Ritual on Vernal Equinox Day}}
{{ill|Gion Flower Stealing Ritual|de|Gion-Blumenraubritual|ja|7月7日 祇園花奪神事|zh|7月7日 祇园夺花仪式|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gion Flower Stealing Ritual|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135578972|1=Gion Flower Stealing Ritual}} | prefecture={{ill|Koka|qid=Q847584|1=Kōka, Shiga}} }} '''Hinō Jinja''' (檜尾神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|qid=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in Kōnan-chō, Ikeda, Kōka City, Shiga Prefecture. == Origins == According to shrine legend, Amatsuhiko-hiko-hono-ninigi-no-mikoto descended to the nearby Taki Pond. Ninigi-no-Mikoto appeared as a blue dragon in the clouds and let the fiery tail hang down, thus being worshipped as "Fire Tail"; fires frequently broke out under this name. After renaming the shrine "Hinō" (Cypress Tail), the fires ceased. The deity is revered for protection against fires and directional misfortune{{Cite web|和書|title=神社紹介 > 桧尾神社|url=http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=614|publisher=滋賀県神社庁|accessdate=2019-09-26}}. == History == Devotion by warlords was strong: the main hall was rebuilt in 1369 (Ōan 2) by Fujiwara Yoriyasu; its roof was replaced by Ikeda Shinjirō in 1525 (Daiei 5); it was renovated by Ikeda Nobuteru and others in 1580 (Tenshō 8); and it was rebuilt in 1707 (Hōei 4). Records show it as the {{ill|Ujigami|en|Ujigami|qid=Q767655|lt=ujigami|1=Ujigami}} of the Ikeda families of both the {{ill|Okayama Domain|en|Okayama Domain|qid=Q903497|lt=Bizen Okayama Domain|1=Okayama Domain}} and the {{ill|Tottori Domain|en|Tottori Domain|qid=Q905521|lt=Inaba Tottori Domain|1=Tottori Domain}}, both of whom originated here, and attest to offerings of prayer tablets made to vassals{{Cite web|和書|title=檜尾神社|url=https://www.biwako-visitors.jp/spot/detail/25234|publisher=公益社団法人びわこビジターズビューロー|accessdate=2019-09-26}}{{Cite book|title=甲賀市史 第8巻 甲賀市事典|date=2016年12月12日|publisher=甲賀市|pages=187-188}}. Before the Meiji Restoration, it was called Hinō Daimyōjin and served as the ujigami of Kami-Ikeda, Shimo-Ikeda, and Taki villages. == Enshrined deities == * Principal deity: Amatsuhiko-hiko-hono-ninigi-no-mikoto * Subsidiary deities: Izanami-no-Mikoto, Susanoo-no-Mikoto, Ebisu-no-Mikoto, Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto, Emperor Ōjin, Sugawara no Michizane == Festivals == * Annual festival – {{ill|Vernal Equinox Day|en|Vernal Equinox Day|qid=Q1049141|lt=Vernal Equinox Day|1=Vernal Equinox Day}} ** Rice Planting Festival of Ikeda – Kōka City designated intangible folk cultural property * Gion Flower Stealing Ritual – July 7 == Subsidiary shrines == * Kotohira Shrine * Yasaka Shrine * Amaterasu Ōmikami Shrine == Cultural properties == === Prefectural designated cultural property === * Main hall – 3×1-bay nagare-zukuri, hinoki bark roof. Built in 1707 (Hōei 4). Restoration completed in 2017 (Heisei 29). === Municipal designated intangible folk cultural property === * Rice Planting Festival of Ikeda == Precincts == File:Hinoo jinja shrine 01.jpg|Torii gate File:Hinoo jinja shrine 03.jpg|Entrance area File:Hinoo jinja shrine 05.jpg|Torii and stone steps File:Hinoo jinja shrine 07.jpg|Rōmon File:Hinoo jinja shrine 09.jpg|Haiden File:Hinoo jinja shrine 13.jpg|Main hall == Other == The stone steps at the entrance were donated in 1466 (Bunshō 1) by Taki no Ryōshu, lord of Taki Province.
Before the Meiji Restoration, Hinō-ji Temple served as the bettō-ji of Hinō Jinja. == Notes == {{commons|Category:Hinoo-jinja}} {{Reflist}} [[ja:檜尾神社]] [[de:Hinō-Schrein]] ==References== {{reflist}} {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{draft categories| [[ja:檜尾神社]] }} {{everybodywiki link|Hinoki Shrine}} [[fr:Hinoki Shrine]] [[zh:Hinoki Shrine]] [[ja:桧尾神社]] {{translated page|ja|檜尾神社|version=102118367|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{Commons category|Hinoo-jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q72184006}} [[Category:Buildings and structures in Koka, Shiga]] [[Category:History of Koka, Shiga]] [[Category:Kanji]] [[Category:Moto-Ise shrines]] [[Category:Municipality Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Properties]] [[Category:Pages linked to by wikidata]] [[Category:Shiga Prefecture designated tangible cultural property]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]
